[2018-07-13 17:37] cogito3 (Cogito): non-prompt defense *does* make the burst strat a lot worse
[2018-07-13 17:37] supergreyman (Greyman): Tarsier: 2-2-2-2
Iso Kronus: 0-8-0-8
[2018-07-13 17:37] cachotognax: in this game I dened absorb by killing hannibulls
[2018-07-13 17:38] cachotognax: so I wanted to start with tarsiers
[2018-07-13 17:38] cogito3 (Cogito): that's fair, when your opponent rushes bulls tarsiers are better
[2018-07-13 17:38] cachotognax: and later iso ws a good attacker, but synching wasn't that huge a deal
[2018-07-13 17:39] cachotognax: I mean sync=dealy, dealy= my opponent can get shredders instead of walls
[2018-07-13 17:39] cogito3 (Cogito): but you could've accomplished the same thing by, say, making 3 isos and getting a militia or gauss charge
[2018-07-13 17:39] cachotognax: I would have killed 1 hannibull per 2 turns then
[2018-07-13 17:39] cogito3 (Cogito): you ultimately lost b/c you didn't ramp up damage quickly enough
[2018-07-13 17:39] cogito3 (Cogito): and tarsiers are really meh when the buildtime isn't denying absorb
[2018-07-13 17:39] cachotognax: I should have transitioned to iso for extra damge, but I was too pressured, and I was too pressured because I misdefended
[2018-07-13 17:40] cogito3 (Cogito): yeah, the major mistake was not getting doomed wall early enough
[2018-07-13 17:40] cogito3 (Cogito): it's possible tarsiers are the right strat against bull rush, I can't speak with certainty on that
[2018-07-13 17:40] cachotognax: iso would have had the same drawback, and the tech for it was a bit more expensive
[2018-07-13 17:40] cogito3 (Cogito): otoh, you wouldn't have had to get the animus so early
[2018-07-13 17:40] cachotognax: not to meantion the extra gold
[2018-07-13 17:41] cachotognax: eh, getting it ealrer migh actualy hve wonme the game
[2018-07-13 17:41] cogito3 (Cogito): bite also has good synergy with iso
[2018-07-13 17:41] cachotognax: that cannon did almost nothing
[2018-07-13 17:41] cogito3 (Cogito): cannon was definitely wrong yeah
[2018-07-13 17:41] cachotognax: ooh bite synergy, that's a good point ofr burst.
[2018-07-13 17:42] cachotognax: animus that turn would have initiated the hannibull slaughter eralier.
[2018-07-13 17:42] willflame: Should I have gotten a second blastforge sometime?
[2018-07-13 17:42] cogito3 (Cogito): probably not? fibroid is pretty good soak
[2018-07-13 17:42] cogito3 (Cogito): I guess you could argue you want to get wall + shredder a turn, but you might be overteched at that point
[2018-07-13 17:42] willflame: After my Hannibull’s started getting killed I just wanted to pump out attack as fast as possible
[2018-07-13 17:43] cogito3 (Cogito): CBBA on like, 15 drones is pretty ambitious
[2018-07-13 17:43] supergreyman (Greyman): Usually 2nd Blastforge for pure defense would normally run you out of Wall supply, but since you also got Shredder and Hannibull and Doomed Wall, it seems ok, but only in midgame-ish.
[2018-07-13 17:43] cachotognax: so the awnser was, you should have gotten a thrid engi and a secon blastforge
[2018-07-13 17:44] cogito3 (Cogito): yeah, on 3 engis you prob want CBBA tech
[2018-07-13 17:44] cachotognax: or neither, but this latter was bad due to the set.
[2018-07-13 17:45] willflame: Also, is it better to soak with Fibroid than Forcefield?
[2018-07-13 17:45] willflame: Or what about engineers?
[2018-07-13 17:45] cogito3 (Cogito): if you have the gold, I think it is. Fibroid doesn't cost a drone and it also threatens for 2
[2018-07-13 17:46] cogito3 (Cogito): fibroid is good soak mainly b/c it threatens for 2
[2018-07-13 17:46] cachotognax: but yeah the saved drone is nice
[2018-07-13 17:46] cachotognax: but my opponent kinda wanted to absorb on the doomed
[2018-07-13 17:47] cachotognax: you meant in case of possible overdefending? well in that case you would just have wanted to attak with it, so I still fall it into meh
[2018-07-13 17:47] cachotognax: I din't have holdable attak.
[2018-07-13 17:47] cogito3 (Cogito): I mean that when you buy fibroid, the opponent has to respect the possibility that you'll absorb on it
[2018-07-13 17:47] cogito3 (Cogito): you can gambit against it, but that still forces out 1 extra defense since you want to gambit for exact
[2018-07-13 17:47] cogito3 (Cogito): it is also good against frostbite in this set
[2018-07-13 17:47] willflame: It’s 2 instant damage for -2 defence next turn though
[2018-07-13 17:48] willflame: I guess that’s ok if that damage gets a breach
[2018-07-13 17:48] cogito3 (Cogito): well, most of the time you end up soaking with it
[2018-07-13 17:48] cogito3 (Cogito): even if it's just 2 instant damage though, if you played around threat w/ it it's still good
[2018-07-13 17:49] supergreyman (Greyman): In this set, Fibroid could be used to gambit Hannibull's click.
[2018-07-13 17:49] cogito3 (Cogito): ah yeah, I forgot about hannibull
[2018-07-13 17:49] cogito3 (Cogito): yes this is a good set for fibroid

[2018-07-16 18:43] _theavatar (The Avatar): Thunderhead is crazily expensive, so it only really gets good value if you get a few turns of damage and absorption. Of course, if buying it is the only way to defend then it’s worth it anyways, but otherwise don’t do it.
[2018-07-16 18:51] cogito3 (Cogito): I've heard shredder t-head is actually pretty decent, and it's worked out for me the one time I did it (vs wincer)
[2018-07-16 18:59] cogito3 (Cogito): so let's think. A t-head that attacks once and then soaks is sort of like two infusion grids and four gauss charges, which is 14GGGGBB. G = 1.33 gold and B = 1.67 gold, so this is 22.66 resources. T-head costs 15GGGGGB, which is 23.32 resources. Considering that t-head soaks for one health more than two infusion grids, I'd say they're roughly equivalent
[2018-07-16 19:01] wilson0862 (Wilson): Presumably their antima is doing more than 11 damage.
[2018-07-16 19:02] cogito3 (Cogito): right, if they're denying absorb it obviously isn't worth it
[2018-07-16 19:02] wilson0862 (Wilson): Nono, I'm saying it will usually soak for the full 11.
[2018-07-16 19:02] wilson0862 (Wilson): You calculated 9hp of soak.
[2018-07-16 19:02] cogito3 (Cogito): I was assuming the two infusions were soaking
[2018-07-16 19:03] wilson0862 (Wilson): They are but igrid soaks for 4.
[2018-07-16 19:03] cogito3 (Cogito): oh damn you're right >_<
[2018-07-16 19:03] wilson0862 (Wilson): So two grids + 1hp is 9.
[2018-07-16 19:03] cogito3 (Cogito): this is what I get for doing mata math right after waking up lol
[2018-07-16 19:03] cogito3 (Cogito): ignore above post, let's redo this
[2018-07-16 19:04] supergreyman (Greyman): You'll probably get a closer approximation if you calculate for 3 grids instead of 2.
[2018-07-16 19:04] cactus_wren (cactus): you can't deny absorb on your comet turn
[2018-07-16 19:04] cactus_wren (cactus): woah discord lagged out
[2018-07-16 19:04] wilson0862 (Wilson): You can buy fewer engis. It's not common for that to be correct but it can be.
[2018-07-16 19:05] cogito3 (Cogito): A t-head that attacks once and then soaks is one health worse than *three* infusion grids and four gauss charges, which is 19GGGGBBB. G = 1.33 gold and B = 1.67 gold, so this is 29.33 resources. T-head costs 15GGGGGB, which is 23.32 resources. One health is worth a lot less than ~6 gold, so the t-head is definitely worth it
[2018-07-16 19:06] cogito3 (Cogito): again, though, this is assuming they can't deny absorb. If they can, it's a lot worse
[2018-07-16 19:07] cogito3 (Cogito): (it should be noted that gauss charge is pretty bad so the t-head is not *as* good as the above math might make it appear)
[2018-07-16 19:07] supergreyman (Greyman): It should be triple B's BTW.
[2018-07-16 19:07] cogito3 (Cogito): I'll just edit the post
[2018-07-16 19:09] cactus_wren (cactus): with prompt absorbers it's impossible, yeah?
[2018-07-16 19:09] cactus_wren (cactus): you buy your engis, they buy their absorbers
[2018-07-16 19:09] cactus_wren (cactus): you can't deny them absorb
[2018-07-16 19:10] cogito3 (Cogito): theoretically I guess you could make your attack in increments of 6 or something so polywall isn't worth
[2018-07-16 19:10] cogito3 (Cogito): but that seems ill-advised
[2018-07-16 19:10] cactus_wren (cactus): i wonder if a unit that recycled engineers the way F recycles drones would be too strong
[2018-07-16 19:10] cogito3 (Cogito): tesla coil is pretty good
[2018-07-16 19:10] cactus_wren (cactus): like recycles engis into bigger blockers, not like tesla coil
[2018-07-16 19:11] terroking: It's good value for the gold, 11 hp and 4 damage is efficient for 15 gold, it's mostly a matter of the opportunity cost of the tons of green
[2018-07-16 19:11] cactus_wren (cactus): in base race type endgames having green lying around to double all your drones' health is so nice
[2018-07-16 19:11] cogito3 (Cogito): making engis into forcefields could probably be balanced if you made it expensive enough
[2018-07-16 19:11] cactus_wren (cactus): probably price it like flexo
[2018-07-16 19:12] cogito3 (Cogito): counting the cost of the drone F is 4G, so if you made the engineer version cost 2G it'd probably be balanced?
[2018-07-16 19:13] cogito3 (Cogito): might even be a little weak tbh, b/c you tend to have a lot more drones lying around than engis
[2018-07-16 19:15] mrguy888: Engineer forcefield already exists.
[2018-07-16 19:15] mrguy888: it is called barrier
[2018-07-16 19:19] mrguy888: Turning a non blocker )mining drone/ into a defender has meaning but turning a blocker into a blocker doesn't. Infusion does it and that ability is rarely used even though there is sense in turning the hp granular. [2018-07-23 03:33] f300xen (f0x): paying 1 drone for 1 threat

[2018-07-23 03:36] namington: but you could argue that SDR should apply to it
[2018-07-23 03:36] namington: or that it shouldn't
[2018-07-23 03:36] namington: ATM, everything currently acts as if SDR applied to it, because nothing benefits you for having lower supply
[2018-07-23 03:36] namington: but its never really said anywhere AFAIK

[2018-07-23 03:37] cogito3 (Cogito): in that sense "low supply = good" is very much in line with SDR ("high supply = good" would violate it)
[2018-07-23 03:37] namington: currently, a position with 10 engineers in supply dominates a position with 9 engineers in supply tho
[2018-07-23 03:37] namington: all else being equal

[2018-07-23 03:39] namington: eh I guess its kinda different but like
[2018-07-23 03:39] namington: its a hard line to draw

[2018-07-23 03:39] namington: "if you have 1 less forcefield, but same forcefield in supply, that means your forcefield died earlier, which means it did something earlier"
[2018-07-23 03:39] Deleted User (Deleted User): supply is definitely a resource
[2018-07-23 03:39] cogito3 (Cogito): it's just meant to avoid degenerate decisions e.g. you don't want ppl to be afraid to kill opp's units
[2018-07-23 03:39] namington: and yeah I would consider supply a "resource"
[2018-07-23 03:39] cogito3 (Cogito): you don't want to be afraid to buy a unit b/c it'll be bad for you

[2018-07-23 03:40] namington: so its not like sdr is
